name: causal-loop-diagram
one_liner: extract variables and signed causal links, identify reinforcing/balancing loops

name: stock-and-flow
one_liner: accumulations and rates of change predict dynamic behavior over time

name: system-archetypes
one_liner: 8 recurring loop patterns: Limits to Growth, Shifting the Burden, Tragedy of Commons, etc.

name: power-laws-scale-free-networks
one_liner: heavy-tail degree distribution from preferential attachment, hubs dominate

name: self-organized-criticality
one_liner: systems tune themselves to critical state, producing power-law avalanches without parameter tuning

name: emergence
one_liner: macroscale patterns irreducible to component-level rules

name: np-hardness
one_liner: computational tractability boundary — if NP-hard, switch to approximation or heuristic

name: policy-cycle
one_liner: 5 stages: agenda-setting → formulation → decision → implementation → evaluation

name: regulatory-impact-analysis
one_liner: compare policy options on cost-benefit and distributional impact, recommend with confidence

name: stakeholder-mapping
one_liner: score each stakeholder on power × interest, derive engagement strategy per quadrant

name: framing-theory
one_liner: meaning is shaped by what is made salient — ask what is foregrounded, naturalized, excluded

name: agenda-setting
one_liner: media doesn't tell you what to think but what to think about — exposure shapes issue salience

name: mcluhan-media-ecology
one_liner: the medium is the message — form shapes cognition more than content

name: mental-accounting
one_liner: people treat money differently by source/intended use, violating fungibility

name: disposition-effect
one_liner: investors sell winners too early and hold losers too long

name: tod
one_liner: score density, mix, walkability, transit access — evaluate TOD viability

name: jacobs-eyes-on-the-street
one_liner: street vitality requires constant informal surveillance by users themselves

name: zoning-theory
one_liner: Euclidean / form-based / inclusionary / performance zoning categories

name: veto-players
one_liner: more / more-distant veto players → less policy change possible

name: electoral-systems
one_liner: FPTP / PR / MMP / runoff / AV — each shapes party system and representation differently

name: state-capacity
one_liner: extractive, administrative, and coercive capacity moderate regime effectiveness

name: van-gennep-rites-of-passage
one_liner: three phases: separation, liminal, incorporation

name: mauss-gift-economy
one_liner: gift exchange creates social obligation networks — give, receive, reciprocate

name: polanyi-reciprocity-redistribution-market
one_liner: three forms of economic integration in human societies

name: liminality
one_liner: threshold states reveal social structure through inversion and produce communitas

name: hebbian-learning
one_liner: neurons that fire together wire together — correlated activity strengthens synapses

name: predictive-processing
one_liner: brain minimizes prediction error via top-down predictions and bottom-up error signals

name: connectome-mapping
one_liner: parcellate brain regions, measure connectivity, compute graph metrics, identify disrupted patterns

name: bioethics-4-principles
one_liner: score Autonomy / Beneficence / Non-maleficence / Justice on the case, resolve conflicts

name: eu-ai-act
one_liner: classify AI system risk tier (unacceptable/high/limited/minimal), check tier obligations

name: nist-ai-rmf
one_liner: walk Govern / Map / Measure / Manage functions to identify and score AI risks

name: gdpr
one_liner: identify personal data, check Art. 6 lawful basis, data subject rights, DPIA necessity

name: oecd-ai-principles
one_liner: 5 principles: inclusive growth, human-centered fairness, transparency, robustness, accountability

name: research-ethics
one_liner: human subjects review: informed consent, risk-benefit, vulnerable population protection, IRB

name: network-analysis
one_liner: compute centrality (degree, betweenness, eigenvector, PageRank), detect communities, interpret structural roles

name: spanning-trees
one_liner: minimum-cost connecting subgraph — characterizes network backbones

name: freudian-psychoanalysis
one_liner: id/ego/superego, repression, Oedipus, dream as wish fulfillment — read symptoms as compromise formations

name: lacanian-psychoanalysis
one_liner: Real/Symbolic/Imaginary, mirror stage, the unconscious is structured like a language, desire of the Other

name: jungian-psychoanalysis
one_liner: collective unconscious, archetypes (shadow, anima/animus, Self), individuation as psychic development

name: toulmin-argument-model
one_liner: extract claim, grounds, warrant, backing, qualifier, rebuttal — assess each component's strength

name: stasis-theory
one_liner: 4 questions of disagreement: conjecture (fact), definition, quality (value), procedure (jurisdiction)

name: classical-canons
one_liner: 5 canons: inventio, dispositio, elocutio, memoria, pronuntiatio

name: peirce-icon-index-symbol
one_liner: 3 sign types by relation to referent: resemblance, causal connection, convention

name: bystander-effect
one_liner: more bystanders → lower individual intervention via diffusion of responsibility

name: situationism
one_liner: situational variance dominates personality variance for behavior — fundamental attribution error

name: social-proof
one_liner: under uncertainty, individuals copy peer behavior — ambiguity and similarity amplify

name: milgram-obedience
one_liner: obedience to authority overrides personal moral resistance under specific conditions

name: schelling-focal-points
one_liner: in coordination games without communication, players converge on culturally salient focal points

name: availability-heuristic
one_liner: probability is judged by ease of recall, not actual frequency

name: confirmation-bias
one_liner: people seek confirming evidence and discount disconfirming evidence

name: anchoring-bias
one_liner: judgments anchor on initially presented numbers regardless of relevance

name: base-rate-fallacy
one_liner: people ignore population base rates when given vivid case-specific information

name: sunk-cost-fallacy
one_liner: past unrecoverable costs irrationally influence forward-looking decisions

name: hindsight-bias
one_liner: known outcomes feel more predictable in retrospect than they were prospectively — 'I knew it all along'
